title: Lesson 35 — Calm and Storm

words.example: The lake was tranquil at dawn, without a ripple.

words.definition: not easily upset or excited; calm and peaceful
words.example: A placid temperament made her ideal for the crisis line.
A serene smile suggested nothing was wrong.

words.definition: denoting a past period regarded as idyllically calm and happy
words.example: He often spoke of the halcyon days before the war.

words.definition: marked by conflict, disorder, or violent change
words.example: The country emerged from a turbulent decade of unrest.

words.definition: characterized by strong and turbulent or conflicting emotion
words.example: Their tempestuous relationship never lasted a calm week.
A boisterous celebration spilled out onto the street.
Even under attack, the CEO stayed unruffled at the podium.

words.definition: having one's feelings and expression under control; calm
words.example: She remained composed through the entire cross-examination.

words.definition: feeling or appearing troubled or nervous
words.example: An agitated crowd pressed toward the locked doors.
A frenzied scramble for the exits followed the alarm.
A hectic morning left no time for breakfast.

story: The island's weather matched its history. Some years brought [[tranquil|tranquil]], [[placid|placid]] seasons — the old-timers called them [[serene|serene]], almost [[halcyon|halcyon]] stretches when nothing much happened. Other years turned [[turbulent|turbulent]] and [[tempestuous|tempestuous]], with [[boisterous|boisterous]] storms rattling the harbor for weeks. The lighthouse keeper prided himself on staying [[unruffled|unruffled]] and [[composed|composed]] through either kind, while newer sailors grew visibly [[agitated|agitated]], sometimes [[frenzied|frenzied]], during even a [[hectic|hectic]] afternoon squall.
